extraction.one_line_summary: GROENEWEGEN Dylan won a wind-shattered sprint after echelons eliminated defending champion Soler and multiple top sprinters including Matthews (crashed).
tactical_patterns: Jumbo-Visma trusted front-loaded leadout for Groenewegen in chaotic wind conditions rather than traditional sprint train positioning.
Team Sky deployed Kwiatkowski to collect intermediate sprint bonuses 3km from finish, revealing GC ambitions beyond sprint stage.
Direct Energie, Delko, and Vital Concept all placed riders in early break, signaling wildcard teams prioritizing visibility.

rider_signals.SOLER Marc: negative: defending champion dropped by echelon splits, losing one minute on opening flat stage reveals poor form
rider_signals.MATTHEWS Michael: negative: crashed out in first race after 2018 season disrupted by broken shoulder, continuity concerns
rider_signals.KWIATKOWSKI Michal: positive: collected 7 total bonus seconds across two intermediate sprints plus KOM points, Sky positioning him for GC
rider_signals.GROENEWEGEN Dylan: positive: third win in 2019 already, positioned himself from right side chaos when Teunissen leadout faded
rider_signals.YATES: neutral: second place suggests strong form but 34-second gap at finish unclear from context
